item.title: Judge tosses federal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: A federal district judge granted Kilmar Abrego Garcia's motion to dismiss two criminal charges on the grounds his prosecution was vindictive and selective.

item.title: Trump's "anti-weaponization fund" faces bipartisan backlash
item.description: President Trump is defending the Justice Department's nearly $1.8 billion so-called anti-weaponization fund, despite heavy criticism from lawmakers on both sides of the aisle. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more from Capitol Hill.

item.title: Staten Island shipyard explosion, fire leaves 16 hurt, including multiple firefighters
item.description: Sixteen people have been hurt in an explosion and fire on a barge at a Staten Island shipyard Friday.

item.title: U.S. prepares for new military strikes against Iran
item.description: At the same time, Tehran is reviewing the latest U.S. proposal for a possible agreement to end the nearly three-month war.

item.title: Why did Tulsi Gabbard step down as director of national intelligence?
item.description: National Intelligence Director Tulsi Gabbard is stepping down from her post effective June 30, citing her husband's battle with cancer. CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro has more details.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Sept. 11 attacks
item.description: In his final tribute to CBS News Radio ahead of its shuttering, Major Garrett introduces coverage of the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.

item.title: What travelers should know as busy Memorial Day weekend begins
item.description: A record number of travelers are set to take to the road and air this Memorial Day Weekend. CBS News senior transportation correspondent Kris Van Cleave has more.

item.title: CIA director brought paramilitary leader involved in Maduro capture to Cuba meeting
item.description: When CIA Director John Ratcliffe visited Havana last week to meet with senior Cuban officials, he brought along one of the covert operators involved in the mission to capture Nicolás Maduro, multiple people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Jon Krakauer reflects on 30 years since surviving deadly Mount Everest climb
item.description: May marks 30 years since the deadly Mount Everest disaster that took the lives of eight people. Journalist Jon Krakauer, who survived the climb, chronicled the tragedy in his bestselling book "Into Thin Air." Krakauer joins to discuss the book's rerelease and his regrets.

item.title: 40,000 evacuated in California chemical leak as officials warn tank may explode
item.description: Southern California officials have expanded an evacuation zone in several Orange County cities over a toxic chemical tank that they say is "actively in crisis."

item.title: Rep. Glenn Ivey and Minnesota AG Keith Ellison on police reform 6 years after George Floyd's murder
item.description: Six years after his murder in Minneapolis, and despite passing the House twice, the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act remains stalled in the Senate. Some lawmakers are hoping to change that. Democratic Rep. Glenn Ivey of Maryland and Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison join to reflect on the state of police reform.

item.title: Trump lawyer-turned-critic Michael Cohen to apply for "anti-weaponization fund"
item.description: ​Michael Cohen, a Trump lawyer-turned-critic, is planning to apply for money from the Justice Department's new "anti-weaponization fund," he told CBS News.

item.title: AI is driving more job cuts and weighing on hiring, economists say
item.description: Economists say AI is reshaping the U.S. labor market by suppressing hiring even as overall job losses remain limited.

item.title: Former Trump lawyer Michael Cohen says he would be "a test case" for DOJ "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: President Trump took to social media Friday to defend the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund despite mounting resistance among some Senate Republicans. Former attorney for Mr. Trump, Michael Cohen, joins to discuss his case for a share of the $1.8 billion compensation fund.

item.title: Gabbard resignation comes as Trump may be considering relaunching Iran war
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard resigned Friday as the director of national intelligence, citing her husband's cancer diagnosis. Gabbard's departure fits within a constellation of events this week suggesting that President Trump may be considering relaunching the war with Iran. CBS News' Aaron MacLean and Samantha Vinograd join with analysis.

item.title: Rubio says Iran imposing tolls on Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable"
item.description: The secretary of state tempered optimism for a deal to end the Iran war, calling Iran's bid to "create a tolling system" in the Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable."

item.title: Kevin Warsh sworn in as new Fed chair, replacing Powell
item.description: Kevin Warsh has vowed to preserve the Fed's independence over monetary policy, telling lawmakers he will never "predetermine" interest rates at the president's request.

item.title: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has "difficult job in front of him," expert says
item.description: President Trump on Friday hosted a swearing-in ceremony for the new Federal Reserve Chair, Kevin Warsh. Jon Hilsenrath, visiting scholar at Duke University, joins with analysis on what Warsh will face in the coming months.

item.title: Stanford professor who studied impact of UFO encounters on brain unpacks new batch of Pentagon files
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released another batch of never-before-seen files on UFOs. Garry Nolan, professor in the Department of Pathology at Stanford University School of Medicine, joins to discuss his study on how witnessing a UFO impacts the brain.

item.title: Weenie 500 winners relish their first-place finish: "It was incredible"
item.description: The Indianapolis 500 is this Memorial Day Weekend, kicked off by the second annual Weenie 500. "Cook 'Em Cam" and "Jack and Cheese," whose New York dog won the race, join CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigning as intel chief, citing husband's cancer diagnosis
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard is resigning as the director of national intelligence after her husband was diagnosed with a rare form of bone cancer.

item.title: Kyle Busch was coughing up blood a day before his death, 911 call reveals
item.description: Kyle Busch's cause of death has not been given, though his family said earlier the NASCAR icon had been hospitalized with a "severe illness."

item.title: Trump administration to require most green card applicants to leave U.S. first
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday announced a sweeping policy designed to make it harder for immigrants already in the U.S. to get permanent residency.

item.title: Jimmie Johnson reacts to Kyle Busch's death: "Lost for words"
item.description: Kyle Busch, 41, died suddenly on Thursday after being hospitalized with an illness, according to his team.

item.title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dies at 41 after a severe illness, his family says
item.description: Kyle Busch's family earlier Thursday announced he had been hospitalized with a "severe illness."

item.title: Stephen Colbert says farewell to "The Late Show" in heartfelt finale
item.description: Stephen Colbert hosted "The Late Show" for the final time Thursday night as the franchise came to an end after 33 years.

item.title: CBS News Radio signing off after nearly 100 years: "An American institution"
item.description: After nearly a century on the air, CBS News Radio is signing off, with the final reports airing on Friday, May 22.

item.title: GOP senators press Blanche on "anti-weaponization fund" in tense meeting
item.description: Some Republican senators openly expressed their concerns about the Justice Department's new "anti-weaponization fund" in a tense meeting Thursday with Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche.

item.title: NASCAR driver Kyle Busch dies at 41 after "severe illness," his family says
item.description: One of the greatest NASCAR racers of all time, Kyle Busch, died on Thursday at 41. His family earlier in the day had said he "experienced a severe illness resulting in hospitalization and was undergoing treatment." Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: 5/21: CBS Evening News
item.description: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies at 41; an Ebola scare causes travel chaos.
